success_surface: Cross-sheet data merging with proper validation, error handling, and range compliance
effective_behaviors: Pre-execution workbook inspection to understand exact structure before modification
Dictionary-based lookup construction for efficient cross-sheet matching
Output directory existence check and creation before file write operations
Strict adherence to specified answer_position cell ranges during iteration
Post-modification verification by reading and displaying updated cell values
Default value assignment for unmatched/missing records per instructions
evidence: First code block loads workbook and prints all sheet headers/data counts to verify structure before solving
get_dict_by_id function creates ID-to-QTY mapping dictionaries for O(1) lookup instead of repeated searches
FileNotFoundError occurred on save, corrected by adding os.makedirs(output_dir, exist_ok=True)
Loop condition included 'if row_idx > 11: break' respecting answer_position ending row
After wb.save(), verification loop printed updated column C and D values with formatting
else clauses assign "-" string when ID not found in lookup dictionaries

memory_items.title: Pre-Execution Workbook Audit
memory_items.lesson: Always inspect all relevant worksheets and their schemas before implementing modifications
memory_items.generalization: Load the workbook and print header names, row counts, and sample data from every affected sheet to detect edge cases like empty headers, trailing rows, or mixed data types

memory_items.title: Lookup Dictionary Pattern
memory_items.lesson: For cross-sheet or cross-table matching, pre-build index dictionaries keyed by matching columns
memory_items.generalization: Iterate through source sheets once to create dict objects mapping primary keys to target values, enabling fast lookups during main processing loops

memory_items.title: Output Path Robustness
memory_items.lesson: Output directory may not exist; always ensure filesystem readiness before writing files
memory_items.generalization: Use os.path.dirname() to extract directory from output path, then call os.makedirs(dir_path, exist_ok=True) before any wb.save() or file write operation

memory_items.title: Answer Range Compliance
memory_items.lesson: Respect explicitly specified cell ranges for answer positions during iteration
memory_items.generalization: When instructions specify answer_position bounds (e.g., C2:D11), include explicit loop guards to stop processing exactly at the defined end row/column

memory_items.title: Verification Before Completion
memory_items.lesson: Read back and display modified cells after saving to confirm updates applied correctly
memory_items.generalization: After saving the workbook, reopen or keep handle and iterate through changed ranges to print values with column headers for sanity checking

memory_items.title: Missing Data Placeholder Strategy
memory_items.lesson: Unmatched records should receive clearly defined default values per task instructions
memory_items.generalization: Implement conditional logic that assigns instruction-specified placeholders (like blank string or dash) when lookup keys do not exist in source data

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Pre-Execution Workbook Audit
- Always inspect all relevant worksheets and their schemas before implementing modifications
- Load the workbook and print header names, row counts, and sample data from every affected sheet to detect edge cases like empty headers, trailing rows, or mixed data types

### Lookup Dictionary Pattern
- For cross-sheet or cross-table matching, pre-build index dictionaries keyed by matching columns
- Iterate through source sheets once to create dict objects mapping primary keys to target values, enabling fast lookups during main processing loops

### Output Path Robustness
- Output directory may not exist; always ensure filesystem readiness before writing files
- Use os.path.dirname() to extract directory from output path, then call os.makedirs(dir_path, exist_ok=True) before any wb.save() or file write operation

### Answer Range Compliance
- Respect explicitly specified cell ranges for answer positions during iteration
- When instructions specify answer_position bounds, include explicit loop guards to stop processing exactly at the defined end row/column

### Verification Before Completion
- Read back and display modified cells after saving to confirm updates applied correctly
- After saving the workbook, reopen or keep handle and iterate through changed ranges to print values with column headers for sanity checking

### Missing Data Placeholder Strategy
- Unmatched records should receive clearly defined default values per task instructions
- Implement conditional logic that assigns instruction-specified placeholders when lookup keys do not exist in source data
